message:
feat(docs): add T6.11 database and messaging migration packs (32 tests)

Migration packs covering before/after patterns for tokio-postgres, sqlx,
mysql_async, rusqlite, redis, async-nats, async-nats jetstream, and rdkafka
to Asupersync equivalents. Includes operational caveats, rollback paths,
troubleshooting decision trees, and evidence links to T6.10/T6.12/T6.13.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{
+  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
+  "document_id": "tokio_t6_migration_packs",
+  "bead_id": "asupersync-2oh2u.6.11",
+  "track": "T6",
+  "title": "Migration Packs: Database and Messaging (Tokio Ecosystem to Asupersync)",
+  "migration_packs": [
+    {
+      "id": "MP-PG-01",
+      "source_crate": "tokio-postgres",
+      "target_module": "asupersync::database::postgres",
+      "feature_gate": "postgres",
+      "categories": ["connection", "query", "transaction", "error_classification", "custom_types"],
+      "api_mappings": 12,
+      "caveats": ["no_background_connection_task", "tosql_signature_differs", "typed_row_accessors"],
+      "status": "complete"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "MP-PG-02",
+      "source_crate": "sqlx (postgres)",
+      "target_module": "asupersync::database::postgres + pool",
+      "feature_gate": "postgres",
+      "categories": ["pool", "query", "derive_replacement", "compile_time_checks"],
+      "api_mappings": 8,
+      "caveats": ["no_derive_from_row", "no_compile_time_query_checking", "sync_pool"],
+      "status": "complete"
+    },
+    {

+      "source_crate": "mysql_async",
+      "target_module": "asupersync::database::mysql",

+      "status": "complete"

+      "target_module": "asupersync::database::mysql + pool",

+    {
+      "id": "MP-RD-01",

+    {
+      "id": "MP-NT-01",
+      "source_crate": "async-nats",
+      "target_module": "asupersync::messaging::nats",
+      "feature_gate": null,
+      "categories": ["connection", "publish", "request_reply", "subscribe"],
+      "api_mappings": 5,

+      "source_crate": "rdkafka",
+      "target_module": "asupersync::messaging::kafka",
+      "feature_gate": "kafka",

+      "api_mappings": 8,
+      "caveats": ["phase0_stubs", "sync_send", "raii_transaction_guard"],
+      "status": "complete"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "MP-POOL-01",
+      "source_crate": "sqlx/deadpool/bb8",
+      "target_module": "asupersync::database::pool",
+      "feature_gate": null,
+      "categories": ["pool_config", "lifecycle", "stats", "retry"],
+      "api_mappings": 10,
+      "caveats": ["sync_mutex_pool", "generic_connection_manager"],
+      "status": "complete"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "MP-RETRY-01",
+      "source_crate": "manual retry loops",
+      "target_module": "asupersync::combinator::retry",
+      "feature_gate": null,
+      "categories": ["retry_policy", "exponential_backoff", "transaction_retry"],
+      "api_mappings": 4,
+      "caveats": [],
+      "status": "complete"
+    }
+  ],
+  "cross_cutting_concerns": {
+    "cancellation": {
+      "tokio_pattern": "CancellationToken / select!",
+      "asupersync_pattern": "&Cx parameter on all async operations",
+      "migration_note": "Replace token checks with Cx-aware operations"
+    },
+    "error_classification": {
+      "tokio_pattern": "Crate-specific error types with manual matching",
+      "asupersync_pattern": "Unified is_transient() / is_retryable() / is_connection_error()",
+      "migration_note": "Replace error code matching with classification methods"
+    },
+    "connection_pooling": {
+      "tokio_pattern": "Async pools (sqlx, deadpool, bb8)",
+      "asupersync_pattern": "Synchronous DbPool<M: ConnectionManager>",
+      "migration_note": "Implement ConnectionManager trait, use sync pool"
+    }
+  },
+  "operational_caveats": [
+    {
+      "id": "CAV-01",
+      "title": "Synchronous pool blocking",
+      "severity": "medium",
+      "description": "DbPool::get() blocks the calling thread. Use try_get() in hot paths.",
+      "workaround": "Use get_with_retry() for fault-tolerant acquisition"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "CAV-02",
+      "title": "Phase 0 Kafka stubs",
+      "severity": "high",
+      "description": "KafkaProducer::send() and Transaction operations are stubs",
+      "workaround": "Continue using rdkafka directly until Phase 1"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "CAV-03",
+      "title": "NATS subscribe stub",
+      "severity": "medium",
+      "description": "NatsClient::subscribe() returns stub error",
+      "workaround": "Use request/reply pattern via client.request()"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "CAV-04",
+      "title": "No derive(FromRow)",
+      "severity": "low",
+      "description": "No compile-time row mapping macros",
+      "workaround": "Implement manual from_row() functions"
+    },
+    {
+      "id": "CAV-05",
+      "title": "NatsClient requires &mut self",
+      "severity": "medium",
+      "description": "Cannot share NatsClient across tasks without Mutex",
+      "workaround": "Create multiple client instances or use Mutex wrapper"
+    }
+  ],
+  "rollback_paths": {
+    "compatibility_crate": "asupersync-tokio-compat",
+    "dual_stack_testing": "Feature-gated test modules",
+    "rollback_steps": [
+      "Revert Cargo.toml dependencies",
+      "Revert import paths",
+      "Remove &cx parameters",
+      "Restore original pool configuration",
+      "Restore original row access patterns"
+    ]
+  },
+  "evidence_links": {
+    "e2e_tests": "tests/e2e_t6_data_path.rs",
+    "unit_tests": "T6.12 (closed)",
+    "integration_tests": "T6.10 (closed)",
+    "postgres_source": "src/database/postgres.rs",
+    "mysql_source": "src/database/mysql.rs",
+    "sqlite_source": "src/database/sqlite.rs",
+    "pool_source": "src/database/pool.rs",
+    "transaction_source": "src/database/transaction.rs",
+    "redis_source": "src/messaging/redis.rs",
+    "nats_source": "src/messaging/nats.rs",
+    "jetstream_source": "src/messaging/jetstream.rs",
+    "kafka_source": "src/messaging/kafka.rs"
+  },
+  "summary": {
+    "total_migration_packs": 12,
+    "source_crates_covered": [
+      "tokio-postgres",
+      "sqlx (postgres, mysql, sqlite)",
+      "mysql_async",
+      "rusqlite",
+      "redis",
+      "async-nats",
+      "async-nats (jetstream)",
+      "rdkafka"
+    ],
+    "total_api_mappings": 87,
+    "total_caveats": 5,
+    "rollback_supported": true
+  },
+  "dependencies": {
+    "blocked_by": [
+      {"bead": "asupersync-2oh2u.6.12", "reason": "Exhaustive unit-test matrix", "status": "closed"},
+      {"bead": "asupersync-2oh2u.6.10", "reason": "Integration/fault-injection suites", "status": "closed"}
+    ],
+    "blocks": [
+      {"bead": "asupersync-2oh2u.11.2", "reason": "Domain-specific migration cookbooks"},
+      {"bead": "asupersync-2oh2u.10.9", "reason": "Replacement-readiness gate aggregator"}
+    ]
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,1241 @@
+# Migration Packs: Database and Messaging (Tokio Ecosystem to Asupersync)
+
+**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.6.11` ([T6.11])
+**Generated by**: SapphireHill (claude-code / opus-4.6)
+**Date**: 2026-03-04
+**Dependencies**: T6.12 (exhaustive unit tests), T6.10 (integration/fault-injection suites)
+**Unblocks**: T9.2 (domain-specific migration cookbooks), T8.9 (replacement-readiness gate)
+**Purpose**: Self-contained migration packs for users moving from Tokio-ecosystem database and messaging crates to Asupersync equivalents.
+
+---
+
+## 1. Prerequisites
+
+Before migrating, ensure:
+
+1. `asupersync` is added to `Cargo.toml` (minimum version: `0.2.7`)
+2. Enable the required feature flags for your backends
+3. For incremental migration, add `asupersync-tokio-compat` with relevant features
+
+### 1.1 Feature Flags
+
+| Feature | Purpose | Required for |
+|---------|---------|-------------|
+| `postgres` | PostgreSQL wire protocol (pure Rust, SCRAM-SHA-256) | sqlx-postgres, tokio-postgres |
+| `mysql` | MySQL wire protocol (pure Rust, auth plugins) | sqlx-mysql, mysql_async |
+| `sqlite` | SQLite via rusqlite blocking pool | sqlx-sqlite, rusqlite |
+| `kafka` | Kafka producer/consumer (rdkafka integration) | rdkafka |
+| *(none)* | Redis, NATS, JetStream — always compiled | redis, async-nats |
+
+### 1.2 Structural Differences
+
+All Asupersync async operations differ from Tokio-ecosystem crates in two ways:
+
+1. **Cancellation context (`&Cx`)**: Every async call takes a `&Cx` parameter for structured cancellation
+2. **Return type (`Outcome<T, E>`)**: Three-state result: `Ok(T)`, `Err(E)`, or cancelled
+
+```rust
+// Tokio pattern:
+let rows = client.query("SELECT 1", &[]).await?;
+
+// Asupersync pattern:
+let rows = conn.query(&cx, "SELECT 1").await?;
+```
+
+---
+
+## 2. PostgreSQL Migration
+
+### 2.1 From `tokio-postgres`
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before (tokio-postgres)**:
+```rust
+use tokio_postgres::{Client, NoTls, Error, Row};
+use tokio_postgres::types::ToSql;
+```
+
+**After (Asupersync)**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::{PgConnection, PgConnectOptions, PgError, PgRow, PgValue};
+use asupersync::database::{ToSql, FromSql, SslMode};
+```
+
+#### Connection
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let (client, connection) = tokio_postgres::connect(
+    "host=localhost user=app dbname=mydb", NoTls
+).await?;
+tokio::spawn(async move { connection.await.unwrap(); });
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let conn = PgConnection::connect(&cx, "postgres://app@localhost/mydb").await?;
+// No background task needed — connection management is internal
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: tokio-postgres requires spawning a background task for the connection driver. Asupersync handles this internally. Remove any `tokio::spawn(connection)` calls.
+
+#### Queries
+
+| tokio-postgres | Asupersync | Notes |
+|---------------|------------|-------|
+| `client.query(sql, &[&p1])` | `conn.query_params(&cx, sql, &[&p1])` | Explicit `_params` suffix |
+| `client.query_one(sql, &[])` | `conn.query_one(&cx, sql)` | Returns `Option<PgRow>` |
+| `client.execute(sql, &[&p1])` | `conn.execute_params(&cx, sql, &[&p1])` | Returns `u64` rows affected |
+| `client.prepare(sql)` | `conn.prepare(&cx, sql)` | Returns `PgStatement` |
+| `client.query(&stmt, &[])` | `conn.query_prepared(&cx, &stmt, &[])` | Execute prepared |
+
+#### Row Access
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let name: String = row.get(0);
+let age: i32 = row.get("age");
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let name = row.get_str("name").unwrap();
+let age = row.get_i32("age").unwrap();
+// Or type-safe:
+let name: String = row.get_typed("name").unwrap();
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: tokio-postgres uses generic `row.get::<_, T>(idx)` with compile-time type inference. Asupersync uses explicit typed accessors (`get_i32`, `get_str`, `get_bool`) or `get_typed::<T>()` via the `FromSql` trait.
+
+#### Transactions
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let tx = client.transaction().await?;
+tx.execute("INSERT INTO ...", &[]).await?;
+tx.commit().await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::with_pg_transaction;
+
+with_pg_transaction(&mut conn, &cx, |tx, cx| async move {
+    tx.execute(cx, "INSERT INTO ...").await?;
+    Ok(())
+}).await?;
+// Auto-commit on Ok, auto-rollback on Err or cancellation
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: Asupersync transactions enforce RAII — the `with_pg_transaction` helper guarantees commit/rollback. Manual `begin`/`commit` is also available but the helper is preferred for correctness.
+
+#### Transaction Retry on Serialization Failure
+
+**Before** (manual retry):
+```rust
+loop {
+    let tx = client.transaction().await?;
+    match do_work(&tx).await {
+        Ok(v) => { tx.commit().await?; return Ok(v); }
+        Err(e) if e.code() == Some(&SqlState::SERIALIZATION_FAILURE) => continue,
+        Err(e) => return Err(e),
+    }
+}
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::with_pg_transaction_retry;
+use asupersync::combinator::RetryPolicy;
+
+let policy = RetryPolicy::default_retry(); // 3 retries, 50ms base, 2s cap
+with_pg_transaction_retry(&mut conn, &cx, &policy, |tx, cx| async move {
+    do_work(tx, cx).await
+}).await?;
+// Automatic retry on SQLSTATE 40001 with exponential backoff
+```
+
+#### Error Classification
+
+| tokio-postgres | Asupersync | Notes |
+|---------------|------------|-------|
+| `e.code() == Some(&SqlState::UNIQUE_VIOLATION)` | `e.is_unique_violation()` | Direct method |
+| `e.code() == Some(&SqlState::SERIALIZATION_FAILURE)` | `e.is_serialization_failure()` | SQLSTATE 40001 |
+| Manual SQLSTATE matching | `e.is_transient()` | Composite check |
+| Manual pattern matching | `e.is_connection_error()` | I/O + class 08 |
+
+#### Custom Types (ToSql/FromSql)
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+impl tokio_postgres::types::ToSql for MyType {
+    fn to_sql(&self, ty: &Type, out: &mut BytesMut)
+        -> Result<IsNull, Box<dyn Error + Send + Sync>> { ... }
+    accepts!(TEXT);
+    to_sql_checked!();
+}
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+impl asupersync::database::ToSql for MyType {
+    fn to_sql(&self) -> (asupersync::database::Format, Vec<u8>, asupersync::database::IsNull) {
+        (Format::Text, self.0.as_bytes().to_vec(), IsNull::No)
+    }
+    fn oid(&self) -> u32 { asupersync::database::pg_oid::TEXT }
+}
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: The `ToSql`/`FromSql` trait signatures differ. Asupersync returns a tuple `(Format, Vec<u8>, IsNull)` instead of writing to `BytesMut`. OID declaration is explicit rather than macro-based.
+
+### 2.2 From `sqlx` (PostgreSQL)
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before (sqlx)**:
+```rust
+use sqlx::postgres::PgPoolOptions;
+use sqlx::{PgPool, Row, FromRow};
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::{PgConnection, PgConnectOptions, PgRow, PgError};
+use asupersync::database::pool::{DbPool, DbPoolConfig, ConnectionManager};
+```
+
+#### Connection Pool
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()
+    .max_connections(20)
+    .min_connections(5)
+    .acquire_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
+    .idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(600))
+    .connect("postgres://app@localhost/mydb")
+    .await?;
+let row = sqlx::query("SELECT 1 AS n").fetch_one(&pool).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+// 1. Define a ConnectionManager implementation
+struct PgManager { url: String }
+
+impl ConnectionManager for PgManager {
+    type Connection = PgConnection;
+    type Error = PgError;
+
+    fn connect(&self) -> Result<PgConnection, PgError> {
+        // Synchronous pool — blocking connect call
+        asupersync::runtime::block_on(|cx| async {
+            PgConnection::connect(&cx, &self.url).await
+        })
+    }
+    fn is_valid(&self, conn: &PgConnection) -> bool {
+        conn.is_connected()
+    }
+}
+
+// 2. Configure pool
+let config = DbPoolConfig::with_max_size(20)
+    .min_idle(5)
+    .connection_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
+    .idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(600))
+    .validate_on_checkout(true);
+
+let pool = DbPool::new(PgManager { url: url.into() }, config);
+pool.warm_up(); // Pre-create min_idle connections
+
+// 3. Acquire and query
+let conn = pool.get()?;
+let rows = conn.query(&cx, "SELECT 1 AS n").await?;
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: sqlx uses an async pool with integrated query execution. Asupersync's `DbPool` is synchronous (uses `std::sync::Mutex`) and generic over any `ConnectionManager`. You must implement `ConnectionManager` for your connection type.
+
+#### `#[derive(FromRow)]` Replacement
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+#[derive(sqlx::FromRow)]
+struct User { id: i64, name: String, email: String }
+
+let user: User = sqlx::query_as("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1")
+    .bind(1i64)
+    .fetch_one(&pool).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+struct User { id: i64, name: String, email: String }
+
+impl User {
+    fn from_row(row: &PgRow) -> Result<Self, PgError> {
+        Ok(Self {
+            id: row.get_i64("id").ok_or_else(|| PgError::Protocol("missing id".into()))?,
+            name: row.get_str("name").unwrap_or_default().to_string(),
+            email: row.get_str("email").unwrap_or_default().to_string(),
+        })
+    }
+}
+
+let row = conn.query_one(&cx, "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1").await?;
+let user = row.map(|r| User::from_row(&r)).transpose()?;
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: Asupersync does not provide derive macros for row mapping. Implement `from_row` manually. This is more verbose but eliminates the compile-time query checking dependency.
+
+#### Compile-Time Query Checking
+
+sqlx's `query!()` and `query_as!()` macros validate SQL against a live database at compile time. Asupersync does not provide this feature. Instead:
+
+- Use `conn.prepare(&cx, sql)` to validate SQL syntax at runtime
+- Write integration tests with `PgConnection::connect` against a test database
+- Use the error classification methods (`is_constraint_violation()`, etc.) for runtime safety
+
+---
+
+## 3. MySQL Migration
+
+### 3.1 From `mysql_async`
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before (mysql_async)**:
+```rust
+use mysql_async::prelude::*;
+use mysql_async::{Pool, Opts, OptsBuilder, Row, Error};
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::{MySqlConnection, MySqlConnectOptions, MySqlError, MySqlRow, MySqlValue};
+use asupersync::database::SslMode; // MySQL variant
+```
+
+#### Connection
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let pool = Pool::new("mysql://user:pass@localhost:3306/mydb");
+let mut conn = pool.get_conn().await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let mut conn = MySqlConnection::connect(&cx, "mysql://user:pass@localhost:3306/mydb").await?;
+// Or with explicit options:
+let opts = MySqlConnectOptions::parse("mysql://user:pass@localhost:3306/mydb")?;
+let mut conn = MySqlConnection::connect_with_options(&cx, opts).await?;
+```
+
+#### Queries
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let rows: Vec<Row> = conn.query("SELECT id, name FROM users").await?;
+let name: String = rows[0].get("name").unwrap();
+
+conn.exec_drop("INSERT INTO users (name) VALUES (?)", ("alice",)).await?;
+let affected = conn.exec_first::<u64, _, _>(
+    "UPDATE users SET name = ? WHERE id = ?", ("bob", 1)
+).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let rows = conn.query(&cx, "SELECT id, name FROM users").await?;
+let name = rows[0].get_str("name").unwrap();
+
+let affected = conn.execute(&cx, "INSERT INTO users (name) VALUES ('alice')").await?;
+let affected = conn.execute(&cx, "UPDATE users SET name = 'bob' WHERE id = 1").await?;
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: mysql_async uses prepared statements by default with tuple parameter binding. Asupersync uses text protocol queries. For parameterized queries, use SQL string formatting with proper escaping (parameterized queries via extended protocol are planned for a future release).
+
+#### Transactions
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let mut tx = conn.start_transaction(Default::default()).await?;
+tx.query_drop("INSERT INTO ...").await?;
+tx.commit().await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::with_mysql_transaction;
+
+with_mysql_transaction(&mut conn, &cx, |tx, cx| async move {
+    tx.execute(cx, "INSERT INTO ...").await?;
+    Ok(())
+}).await?;
+```
+
+#### Error Classification
+
+| mysql_async | Asupersync | Notes |
+|------------|------------|-------|
+| `ServerError { code: 1062, .. }` | `e.is_unique_violation()` | ER_DUP_ENTRY |
+| `ServerError { code: 1213, .. }` | `e.is_deadlock()` | Also covers 1205 (lock wait) |
+| Manual error code matching | `e.is_transient()` | Composite check |
+| `e.server_error()` | `e.server_code()` | Raw error number |
+
+#### Transaction Retry on Deadlock
+
+**Before** (manual):
+```rust
+loop {
+    match do_transaction(&pool).await {
+        Ok(v) => return Ok(v),
+        Err(e) if e.server_error().map_or(false, |se| se.code == 1213) => continue,
+        Err(e) => return Err(e),
+    }
+}
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::with_mysql_transaction_retry;
+use asupersync::combinator::RetryPolicy;
+
+with_mysql_transaction_retry(&mut conn, &cx, &RetryPolicy::default_retry(),
+    |tx, cx| async move { do_work(tx, cx).await }
+).await?;
+// Automatic retry on deadlock (1213/1205) with exponential backoff
+```
+
+### 3.2 From `sqlx` (MySQL)
+
+The pool setup follows the same pattern as PostgreSQL (Section 2.2) but with `MySqlConnection` as the connection type and `MySqlError` as the error type.
+
+---
+
+## 4. SQLite Migration
+
+### 4.1 From `sqlx` (SQLite) / `rusqlite`
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before (sqlx-sqlite)**:
+```rust
+use sqlx::sqlite::SqlitePoolOptions;
+use sqlx::{SqlitePool, Row};
+```
+
+**Before (rusqlite)**:
+```rust
+use rusqlite::{Connection, params};
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::{SqliteConnection, SqliteError, SqliteRow, SqliteValue};
+```
+
+#### Connection
+
+**Before (sqlx)**:
+```rust
+let pool = SqlitePoolOptions::new()
+    .max_connections(5)
+    .connect("sqlite:mydb.sqlite").await?;
+```
+
+**Before (rusqlite)**:
+```rust
+let conn = Connection::open("mydb.sqlite")?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let conn = SqliteConnection::open(&cx, "mydb.sqlite").await?;
+// Or in-memory:
+let conn = SqliteConnection::open_in_memory(&cx).await?;
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: Asupersync uses a blocking pool internally for SQLite operations. The async interface is thin — `open` dispatches to a blocking thread. WAL mode and foreign keys are enabled by default.
+
+#### Queries
+
+**Before (sqlx)**:
+```rust
+let row = sqlx::query("SELECT name FROM users WHERE id = ?")
+    .bind(1i64)
+    .fetch_one(&pool).await?;
+let name: String = row.get("name");
+```
+
+**Before (rusqlite)**:
+```rust
+let name: String = conn.query_row(
+    "SELECT name FROM users WHERE id = ?1",
+    params![1i64],
+    |row| row.get(0),
+)?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let rows = conn.query(&cx, "SELECT name FROM users WHERE id = ?1", &["1"]).await?;
+let name = rows.first().and_then(|r| r.get_str("name")).unwrap();
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: SQLite parameters use `?1`, `?2` positional syntax. Parameters are passed as string slices — Asupersync handles SQLite's type affinity system. For typed access, use `get_i64`, `get_f64`, `get_str`, or `get_blob`.
+
+#### Batch Execution
+
+**Before (rusqlite)**:
+```rust
+conn.execute_batch("
+    CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);
+    CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER);
+")?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+conn.execute_batch(&cx, "
+    CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT);
+    CREATE TABLE posts (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER);
+").await?;
+```
+
+#### Transactions
+
+**Before (rusqlite)**:
+```rust
+let tx = conn.transaction()?;
+tx.execute("INSERT INTO users (name) VALUES (?1)", params!["alice"])?;
+tx.commit()?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::with_sqlite_transaction;
+
+with_sqlite_transaction(&mut conn, &cx, |tx, cx| async move {
+    tx.execute(cx, "INSERT INTO users (name) VALUES (?1)", &["alice"]).await?;
+    Ok(())
+}).await?;
+
+// Or with immediate write lock:
+use asupersync::database::with_sqlite_transaction_immediate;
+with_sqlite_transaction_immediate(&mut conn, &cx, |tx, cx| async move {
+    tx.execute(cx, "UPDATE counters SET n = n + 1", &[]).await?;
+    Ok(())
+}).await?;
+```
+
+#### Transaction Retry on BUSY/LOCKED
+
+**Before** (manual retry with busy handler):
+```rust
+conn.busy_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))?;
+// Or manual retry loop
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::with_sqlite_transaction_retry;
+
+// Busy timeout is also available:
+conn.set_busy_timeout(&cx, Duration::from_secs(5)).await?;
+
+// Or structured retry:
+with_sqlite_transaction_retry(&mut conn, &cx, &RetryPolicy::default_retry(),
+    |tx, cx| async move { do_work(tx, cx).await }
+).await?;
+// Retries on SQLITE_BUSY and SQLITE_LOCKED
+```
+
+#### Savepoints
+
+All three backends (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite) support savepoints for nested transactions:
+
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::PgSavepoint; // or SqliteSavepoint, MySqlSavepoint
+
+let tx = conn.begin(&cx).await?;
+let sp = PgSavepoint::new(&tx, &cx, "my_savepoint").await?;
+// Do nested work...
+sp.release(&cx).await?; // or sp.rollback(&cx).await?
+tx.commit(&cx).await?;
+```
+
+---
+
+## 5. Redis Migration
+
+### 5.1 From `redis` crate
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before (redis)**:
+```rust
+use redis::{Client, AsyncCommands, RedisError};
+use redis::aio::MultiplexedConnection;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::messaging::{RedisClient, RedisConfig, RedisError};
+```
+
+#### Connection
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let client = redis::Client::open("redis://localhost:6379/")?;
+let mut conn = client.get_multiplexed_async_connection().await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let client = RedisClient::connect(&cx, "redis://localhost:6379/").await?;
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: The redis crate separates `Client` (config) from `Connection` (I/O). Asupersync's `RedisClient` combines both with internal connection pooling.
+
+#### Commands
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let _: () = conn.set("key", "value").await?;
+let val: String = conn.get("key").await?;
+let _: () = conn.del("key").await?;
+let n: i64 = conn.incr("counter", 1).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+client.cmd(&cx, &["SET", "key", "value"]).await?;
+let val = client.get(&cx, "key").await?; // Returns Option<Vec<u8>>
+client.cmd(&cx, &["DEL", "key"]).await?;
+client.cmd(&cx, &["INCR", "counter", "1"]).await?;
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: The redis crate provides typed convenience methods (`set`, `get`, `del`, `incr`). Asupersync uses `cmd()` with string argument arrays for generic commands, plus `get()` as a typed convenience. Response values are `RespValue` enums that need manual extraction.
+
+#### Response Handling
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let val: String = conn.get("key").await?;
+let vals: Vec<String> = conn.lrange("list", 0, -1).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::messaging::redis::RespValue;
+
+let resp = client.cmd(&cx, &["GET", "key"]).await?;
+let val = match resp {
+    RespValue::BulkString(Some(bytes)) => String::from_utf8(bytes).ok(),
+    _ => None,
+};
+
+let resp = client.cmd(&cx, &["LRANGE", "list", "0", "-1"]).await?;
+let vals = match resp {
+    RespValue::Array(Some(items)) => items.iter().filter_map(|v| {
+        if let RespValue::BulkString(Some(b)) = v { String::from_utf8(b.clone()).ok() }
+        else { None }
+    }).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
+    _ => vec![],
+};
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: The redis crate auto-deserializes responses via `FromRedisValue`. Asupersync returns raw `RespValue` that must be pattern-matched. This is more explicit but requires more code at call sites.
+
+#### Error Classification
+
+| redis crate | Asupersync | Notes |
+|------------|------------|-------|
+| `RedisError::is_io_error()` | `e.is_connection_error()` | Includes I/O |
+| `RedisError::is_connection_refusal()` | `e.is_connection_error()` | Composite |
+| Manual `kind()` matching | `e.is_transient()` | I/O + pool exhausted |
+| N/A | `e.is_capacity_error()` | Pool exhaustion |
+
+---
+
+## 6. NATS Migration
+
+### 6.1 From `async-nats`
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before (async-nats)**:
+```rust
+use async_nats::{Client, ConnectOptions, Message};
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::messaging::{NatsClient, NatsConfig, NatsError, NatsMessage};
+```
+
+#### Connection
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let client = async_nats::connect("nats://localhost:4222").await?;
+// Or with options:
+let client = async_nats::ConnectOptions::new()
+    .user_and_password("user".into(), "pass".into())
+    .connect("nats://localhost:4222").await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let mut client = NatsClient::connect(&cx, "nats://localhost:4222").await?;
+// Or with config:
+let config = NatsConfig::from_url("nats://user:pass@localhost:4222")?;
+let mut client = NatsClient::connect_with_config(&cx, config).await?;
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: `NatsClient` requires `&mut self` for publish/subscribe (single-owner pattern). The async-nats client is `Clone + Send` for shared usage. If you need concurrent publishers, create multiple client instances or use a `Mutex`.
+
+#### Publish/Subscribe
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+client.publish("subject", "payload".into()).await?;
+
+let mut subscriber = client.subscribe("subject").await?;
+while let Some(msg) = subscriber.next().await {
+    println!("Received: {:?}", msg.payload);
+}
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+client.publish(&cx, "subject", b"payload").await?;
+
+// Subscribe is Phase 0 — returns stub error
+// Use request/reply pattern instead:
+let reply = client.request(&cx, "subject", b"request").await?;
+println!("Reply: {:?}", reply.payload);
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: NATS subscription is currently a stub (Phase 0). For request/reply patterns, use `client.request()` which auto-generates inbox subjects and handles response routing. Full pub/sub subscription will be available in a future release.
+
+#### Request/Reply
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let response = client.request("service.endpoint", "request".into()).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let response = client.request(&cx, "service.endpoint", b"request").await?;
+// response.subject, response.payload, response.reply_to
+```
+
+---
+
+## 7. JetStream Migration
+
+### 7.1 From `async-nats` JetStream
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+use async_nats::jetstream;
+use async_nats::jetstream::consumer::PullConsumer;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::messaging::{
+    JetStreamContext, StreamConfig, ConsumerConfig,
+    AckPolicy, DeliverPolicy, RetentionPolicy, StorageType, DiscardPolicy,
+    PubAck, JsMessage, StreamInfo, JsError,
+};
+```
+
+#### Context Creation
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let client = async_nats::connect("nats://localhost:4222").await?;
+let jetstream = async_nats::jetstream::new(client);
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let client = NatsClient::connect(&cx, "nats://localhost:4222").await?;
+let mut js = JetStreamContext::new(client);
+// Or with custom API prefix:
+let mut js = JetStreamContext::with_prefix(client, "$JS.MYACCOUNT.API");
+```
+
+#### Stream Management
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let stream = jetstream.create_stream(jetstream::stream::Config {
+    name: "orders".into(),
+    subjects: vec!["orders.>".into()],
+    retention: RetentionPolicy::Limits,
+    storage: StorageType::File,
+    max_messages: 1_000_000,
+    ..Default::default()
+}).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let config = StreamConfig::new("orders")
+    .subjects(vec!["orders.>".into()])
+    .retention(RetentionPolicy::Limits)
+    .storage(StorageType::File)
+    .max_messages(1_000_000);
+
+let info = js.create_stream(&cx, config).await?;
+```
+
+#### Publishing
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let ack = jetstream.publish("orders.new", payload.into()).await?.await?;
+// Note: double-await — first sends, second waits for ack
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let ack = js.publish(&cx, "orders.new", payload).await?;
+// Single await — returns PubAck with stream, seq, duplicate fields
+assert!(!ack.duplicate);
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: async-nats JetStream publish returns a `PublishAckFuture` that requires a second `.await` for the server acknowledgement. Asupersync combines send + ack into a single await.
+
+#### Consumer Management
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let consumer = stream.create_consumer(jetstream::consumer::pull::Config {
+    durable_name: Some("processor".into()),
+    ack_policy: AckPolicy::Explicit,
+    ..Default::default()
+}).await?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let config = ConsumerConfig::new("processor")
+    .deliver_policy(DeliverPolicy::All)
+    .ack_policy(AckPolicy::Explicit)
+    .ack_wait(Duration::from_secs(30))
+    .max_deliver(5);
+
+let consumer = js.create_consumer(&cx, "orders", config).await?;
+```
+
+#### Error Classification
+
+| async-nats JetStream | Asupersync | Notes |
+|---------------------|------------|-------|
+| `PublishError::*` | `JsError::Api { code, .. }` | API error with code |
+| Stream not found | `JsError::StreamNotFound(name)` | Explicit variant |
+| Consumer not found | `JsError::ConsumerNotFound { .. }` | Explicit variant |
+| Config validation | `JsError::InvalidConfig(msg)` | Config errors |
+| Timeout (implicit) | `JsError::Api { code: 408, .. }` | Request timeout |
+| N/A | `e.is_transient()` | Code 503/408 or NotAcked |
+
+---
+
+## 8. Kafka Migration
+
+### 8.1 From `rdkafka`
+
+#### Import Changes
+
+**Before (rdkafka)**:
+```rust
+use rdkafka::producer::{FutureProducer, FutureRecord};
+use rdkafka::consumer::{StreamConsumer, Consumer};
+use rdkafka::ClientConfig;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::messaging::{
+    KafkaProducer, ProducerConfig, Compression, Acks,
+    KafkaConsumer, KafkaConsumerConfig, AutoOffsetReset,
+    RecordMetadata, KafkaError,
+};
+```
+
+#### Producer
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let producer: FutureProducer = ClientConfig::new()
+    .set("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092")
+    .set("message.timeout.ms", "5000")
+    .create()?;
+
+let record = FutureRecord::to("topic")
+    .key("key")
+    .payload("value");
+let (partition, offset) = producer.send(record, Duration::from_secs(5)).await??;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let config = ProducerConfig::new(vec!["localhost:9092".into()])
+    .acks(Acks::All)
+    .compression(Compression::Snappy)
+    .enable_idempotence(true)
+    .retries(3);
+
+let producer = KafkaProducer::new(config)?;
+let metadata = producer.send(
+    &cx, "topic", Some(b"key"), b"value", None
+)?;
+// metadata.topic, metadata.partition, metadata.offset
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: rdkafka wraps librdkafka (C library) and provides a full-featured async producer. Asupersync's Kafka integration is Phase 0 — send operations are synchronous stubs that will be backed by rdkafka in a future release. The API surface is stable.
+
+#### Transactional Producer
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let producer: FutureProducer = ClientConfig::new()
+    .set("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092")
+    .set("transactional.id", "my-tx")
+    .create()?;
+producer.init_transactions(Duration::from_secs(10))?;
+producer.begin_transaction()?;
+producer.send(record, Duration::from_secs(5)).await??;
+producer.commit_transaction(Duration::from_secs(10))?;
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::messaging::{TransactionalProducer, TransactionalConfig, Transaction};
+
+let config = TransactionalConfig::new()
+    .transaction_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60));
+// Note: transaction_id is auto-generated
+
+let tx_producer = TransactionalProducer::new(config)?;
+let tx = tx_producer.begin_transaction(&cx)?;
+tx.send(&cx, "topic", Some(b"key"), b"value")?;
+tx.commit(&cx).await?;
+// Or tx.abort(&cx).await? on failure
+```
+
+> **Caveat**: Phase 0 — transactional operations are stubs. The RAII `Transaction` guard warns on drop if not explicitly committed/aborted.
+
+#### Consumer
+
+**Before**:
+```rust
+let consumer: StreamConsumer = ClientConfig::new()
+    .set("bootstrap.servers", "localhost:9092")
+    .set("group.id", "my-group")
+    .set("auto.offset.reset", "earliest")
+    .create()?;
+consumer.subscribe(&["topic"])?;
+
+while let Some(msg) = consumer.recv().await? {
+    let payload = msg.payload().unwrap();
+    // process...
+}
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+let config = KafkaConsumerConfig::new(
+    vec!["localhost:9092".into()],
+    "my-group".into(),
+)
+.auto_offset_reset(AutoOffsetReset::Earliest);
+
+let consumer = KafkaConsumer::new(config)?;
+// Consumer API follows same pattern — Phase 0 stubs
+```
+
+---
+
+## 9. Connection Pool Migration
+
+### 9.1 Generic Pool Pattern
+
+All Asupersync database backends use the same generic pool framework:
+
+```rust
+use asupersync::database::pool::{DbPool, DbPoolConfig, DbPoolError, ConnectionManager};
+
+// Step 1: Implement ConnectionManager for your backend
+impl ConnectionManager for MyManager {
+    type Connection = MyConn;
+    type Error = MyError;
+    fn connect(&self) -> Result<MyConn, MyError> { ... }
+    fn is_valid(&self, conn: &MyConn) -> bool { ... }
+    fn disconnect(&self, _conn: MyConn) { /* optional cleanup */ }
+}
+
+// Step 2: Configure
+let config = DbPoolConfig::with_max_size(20)
+    .min_idle(5)
+    .validate_on_checkout(true)
+    .idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(600))
+    .max_lifetime(Duration::from_secs(3600))
+    .connection_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30));
+
+// Step 3: Create and warm up
+let pool = DbPool::new(MyManager::new(), config);
+pool.warm_up();
+
+// Step 4: Use
+let conn = pool.get()?;              // Blocking acquire
+let conn = pool.try_get()?;          // Non-blocking
+let conn = pool.get_with_retry(&RetryPolicy::default_retry())?; // With backoff
+
+// Step 5: Observe
+let stats = pool.stats();
+// stats.idle, stats.active, stats.total, stats.total_acquisitions, etc.
+```
+
+### 9.2 Pool Config Equivalents
+
+| sqlx / deadpool / bb8 | Asupersync DbPoolConfig | Notes |
+|----------------------|------------------------|-------|
+| `max_connections(n)` | `with_max_size(n)` | Same semantics |
+| `min_connections(n)` | `min_idle(n)` | Pre-created idle connections |
+| `acquire_timeout(d)` | `connection_timeout(d)` | Max wait for acquire |
+| `idle_timeout(d)` | `idle_timeout(d)` | Same |
+| `max_lifetime(d)` | `max_lifetime(d)` | Same |
+| `test_before_acquire(true)` | `validate_on_checkout(true)` | Health check on acquire |
+
+### 9.3 Pool Lifecycle
+
+```rust
+// Return connection to pool (default on drop):
+drop(conn);  // or conn.return_to_pool();
+
+// Discard unhealthy connection:
+conn.discard();  // Does not return to pool
+
+// Evict stale connections:
+pool.evict_stale();
+
+// Graceful shutdown:
+pool.close();
+assert!(pool.is_closed());
+```
+
+---
+
+## 10. Retry Policy Migration
+
+### 10.1 From Manual Retry Loops
+
+**Before** (common tokio pattern):
+```rust
+let mut attempts = 0;
+loop {
+    match do_operation().await {
+        Ok(v) => return Ok(v),
+        Err(e) if e.is_transient() && attempts < 3 => {
+            attempts += 1;
+            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50 * 2u64.pow(attempts))).await;
+        }
+        Err(e) => return Err(e),
+    }
+}
+```
+
+**After**:
+```rust
+use asupersync::combinator::{RetryPolicy, calculate_delay};
+
+// Pre-built policies:
+let policy = RetryPolicy::default_retry();  // 3 retries, 50ms base, 2s cap
+let policy = RetryPolicy::none();           // No retries
+
+// Custom policy:
+let policy = RetryPolicy {
+    max_retries: 5,
+    base_delay: Duration::from_millis(100),
+    max_delay: Duration::from_secs(10),
+};
+
+// Used with pool:
+let conn = pool.get_with_retry(&policy)?;
+
+// Used with transactions:
+with_pg_transaction_retry(&mut conn, &cx, &policy, |tx, cx| async move {
+    do_work(tx, cx).await
+}).await?;
+```
+
+---
+
+## 11. Operational Caveats
+
+### 11.1 Cancellation Model
+
+All Asupersync operations honour structured cancellation via `&Cx`. When a `Cx` is cancelled:
+
+- Active queries will be interrupted and return `Err` (or `Outcome::Cancelled`)
+- Pool acquisitions waiting in queue will be cancelled
+- Transaction helpers automatically rollback on cancellation
+- JetStream unacked messages will be redelivered by the server
+
+**Action**: Ensure your error handling distinguishes between operational errors and cancellation. Use `match outcome { Outcome::Cancelled => ..., ... }` where needed.
+
+### 11.2 Synchronous Pool
+
+Unlike sqlx/deadpool/bb8 which use async pools, `DbPool` uses `std::sync::Mutex`:
+
+- **Advantage**: No runtime dependency for pool management; works in any context
+- **Constraint**: `pool.get()` blocks the calling thread during acquire
+- **Recommendation**: Use `pool.try_get()` in hot paths where blocking is unacceptable, or use `pool.get_with_retry()` for fault-tolerant acquisition
+
+### 11.3 Phase 0 Stubs
+
+The following APIs are Phase 0 stubs (API-stable but return stub errors):
+
+| Module | Stub Operations |
+|--------|----------------|
+| Kafka | `KafkaProducer::send()`, `Transaction::commit/abort()` |
+| NATS | `NatsClient::subscribe()` |
+| JetStream | `publish_with_id()` (dedup via Nats-Msg-Id header) |
+
+**Recommendation**: For Kafka, continue using rdkafka directly until Phase 1 integration. For NATS pub/sub, use `request()`/`publish()` which are fully functional.
+
+### 11.4 Type System Differences
+
+| Concern | Tokio Ecosystem | Asupersync |
+|---------|----------------|------------|
+| Row mapping | `#[derive(FromRow)]` (sqlx) | Manual `from_row()` impl |
+| Query checking | `query!()` compile-time (sqlx) | Runtime `prepare()` |
+| Parameter binding | Generic `.bind(val)` | Explicit `&[&dyn ToSql]` or string slices |
+| Error types | Crate-specific | Unified `is_transient()`/`is_retryable()` |
+| Connection driver | Background task (tokio-postgres) | Internal management |
+
+---
+
+## 12. Rollback Paths
+
+### 12.1 Incremental Migration via Compatibility Crate
+
+For projects that cannot migrate all at once, `asupersync-tokio-compat` provides bridge adapters:
+
+```toml
+[dependencies]
+asupersync-tokio-compat = { version = "0.1", features = ["postgres", "mysql"] }
+```
+
+This allows running Asupersync operations within a Tokio runtime context and vice versa.
+
+### 12.2 Rollback Checklist
+
+If migration encounters issues:
+
+1. **Revert `Cargo.toml`**: Remove `asupersync` features, restore original crate dependencies
+2. **Revert import paths**: `asupersync::database::*` back to `tokio_postgres::*` / `sqlx::*` / etc.
+3. **Restore async patterns**: Remove `&cx` parameters, revert to `?` error handling
+4. **Restore pool config**: Replace `DbPoolConfig` with original pool options
+5. **Restore type mapping**: Replace `get_i32()`/`get_str()` with original row access patterns
+
+### 12.3 Dual-Stack Testing
+
+During migration, run tests against both stacks:
+
+```rust
+#[cfg(feature = "asupersync-backend")]
+mod asupersync_tests {
+    use asupersync::database::*;
+    // Asupersync-based tests
+}
+
+#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-backend"))]
+mod legacy_tests {
+    use tokio_postgres::*;
+    // Original tests
+}
+```
+
+---
+
+## 13. Troubleshooting Decision Points
+
+### 13.1 Connection Failures
+
+```
+Symptom: PgError::Io / MySqlError::Io / RedisError::Io
+├── Check: Is the database/service reachable? (network, firewall, DNS)
+├── Check: Are credentials correct? (PgConnectOptions, MySqlConnectOptions)
+├── Check: Is SSL/TLS configured correctly? (SslMode)
+└── Action: Use pool.get_with_retry() with appropriate RetryPolicy
+```
+
+### 13.2 Pool Exhaustion
+
+```
+Symptom: DbPoolError::Full / DbPoolError::Timeout
+├── Check: pool.stats() — are all connections active?
+├── Check: Are connections being returned? (RAII drop or explicit return_to_pool)
+├── Check: Is max_size adequate for your concurrency level?
+├── Action: Increase max_size, add idle_timeout, enable validate_on_checkout
+└── Action: Use pool.evict_stale() to clean dead connections
+```
+
+### 13.3 Transaction Deadlocks
+
+```
+Symptom: PgError::is_deadlock() / MySqlError::is_deadlock()
+├── Check: Are transactions acquiring locks in consistent order?
+├── Check: Are transaction scopes minimal (hold locks briefly)?
+├── Action: Use with_*_transaction_retry() for automatic backoff
+└── Action: Review query order within transactions
+```
+
+### 13.4 SQLite Busy/Locked
+
+```
+Symptom: SqliteError::is_busy() / SqliteError::is_locked()
+├── Check: Is WAL mode enabled? (default: yes)
+├── Check: Is busy_timeout set? (default: 250ms)
+├── Check: Multiple writers? (SQLite allows one writer at a time)
+├── Action: Use with_sqlite_transaction_immediate() for write transactions
+└── Action: Increase busy_timeout or use with_sqlite_transaction_retry()
+```
+
+---
